name: Graham Wafers
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Graham flour (or whole wheat flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the margarine, sugar, and vanilla extract until smooth.
2. Gradually add the dry ingredients (Graham flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt) to the butter mixture. Stir with a spoon or beat on low until the mixture is crumbly but holds together when formed into a ball.
3. Add the milk and stir until incorporated. The batter should be moist but not runny.
4. Place a sheet of parchment paper on a lightly floured surface. Roll out the dough to about 1/8 inch (or less) thickness, using a ruler for even cutting.
5. Cut into squares measuring approximately 2 1/2 inches each.
6. Arrange the cut wafers on ungreased baking sheets without leaving space between them. Prick evenly with a fork.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 10 to 12 minutes, or until lightly golden brown. Remove from the baking sheet while still hot.
This recipe yields approximately three dozen wafers. Each wafer contains around 42 calories, trace cholesterol, and 44 mg of sodium, with 1 gram of fat.
